metadata.name: Microsoft Fabric Retail Demo
metadata.description: Cross-domain metadata for the Fabric-native retail demo.

prerequisites.name: Microsoft Fabric access
prerequisites.description: A Fabric tenant, capacity, and permissions for the target workspace.

prerequisites.name: Git
prerequisites.description: Version control client used to obtain and manage the repository.

prerequisites.name: Python
prerequisites.description: Python runtime for bootstrap and the retail-setup utility.

prerequisites.name: Terraform
prerequisites.description: Infrastructure client used by the supported deployment workflow.

prerequisites.name: Azure CLI
prerequisites.description: Default identity client and the identity client installed by guided bootstrap.

prerequisites.name: SQL Server ODBC driver
prerequisites.description: ODBC Driver 17 or 18 for SQL Server enables Lakehouse SQL freshness evidence.

prerequisites.name: Azure PowerShell
prerequisites.description: Optional local identity fallback when Azure CLI credentials are unavailable.

commands.name: Guided bootstrap
commands.description: Prepare dependencies, configure, render, and optionally deploy.

commands.name: Configure
commands.description: Create target and generation configuration for one workspace environment.

commands.name: Render
commands.description: Render workspace-specific setup and streaming notebooks.

commands.name: Deploy
commands.description: Run the supported target-aware Fabric deployment workflow.

commands.name: Verify readiness
commands.description: Evaluate the fixed local/live readiness taxonomy for a resolved deployment profile.

profiles.name: Core
profiles.description: Default GA-safe historical deployment with no preview, live, ML, reporting, or destructive surfaces.

boundaries.runtime: Publishes the four historical setup notebooks; the operator runs them in order after deployment.
boundaries.capacity: Uses the workspace starter pool and does not provision Eventhouse or a custom Spark pool.
boundaries.cost: Limits automatic deployment to the workspace, Lakehouse, and historical setup notebooks; Fabric usage still incurs tenant-specific charges.
boundaries.manual: Historical notebook execution and validation remain operator actions; no schedule or live stream is enabled.
boundaries.preview: No known preview or undocumented deployment surface is selected.

profiles.name: Standard
profiles.description: Supported historical and real-time path with required ML and fail-closed Reporting publication.

boundaries.runtime: Publishes infrastructure first, waits for setup and required ML validation, then publishes Reporting; no schedule or live stream is enabled.
boundaries.capacity: Requires a Fabric capacity that supports Lakehouse, Eventhouse, KQL, Spark, pipelines, and Power BI; it uses the starter Spark pool.
boundaries.cost: Eventhouse, streaming, Spark, ML, and reporting can consume more capacity than core; actual charges depend on the tenant and run duration.
boundaries.manual: The stream notebook remains operator-controlled; required setup and ML gates run synchronously during deployment.
boundaries.preview: Ontology, Data Agents, custom Spark pool, and task-flow metadata deployment are excluded.

profiles.name: Full demo
profiles.description: All advanced and preview demo surfaces, with optional and experimental ML isolated from the required Reporting gate.

required_acknowledgements.description: The operator verified that the tenant permits Ontology and Data Agent preview surfaces.
The operator reviewed the configured custom Spark pool against the target capacity.
The operator accepts that task-flow deployment uses an undocumented metadata endpoint.
The operator accepts that dashboard templates, rule definitions, stream execution, and final bindings require manual work.

boundaries.runtime: Publishes infrastructure, waits for setup and required ML validation, publishes Reporting, then runs isolated optional and experimental ML pipelines.
boundaries.capacity: Requires operator-reviewed capacity for concurrent Eventhouse, Spark, custom-pool, Power BI, ontology, and agent workloads.
boundaries.cost: Selects every non-destructive demo surface and therefore has the broadest potential Fabric consumption; no numeric estimate is asserted.
boundaries.manual: Dashboard and Activator sources are not auto-published, the stream is manually controlled, and preview bindings may need operator repair.
boundaries.preview: Ontology, Data Agents, custom Spark pool, and undocumented task-flow metadata behavior require explicit acknowledgements.

data_contracts.name: Live event contract
data_contracts.description: Declaration linking Eventhouse DDL, JSON mappings, and direct-writer payload metadata.

inventories.description: Eventhouse tables derived from KQL DDL.
Mapped event tables derived from KQL ingestion mappings.
Generated business event types derived from EVENT_PAYLOADS.

inventory_rules.description: KQL DDL and ingestion mappings must declare the same tables.

source_agreements.description: DDL, mappings, envelope, and payload fields and types must agree.

event_time.description: Envelope ingestion time is the sale occurrence time.
Envelope ingestion time orders receipt lines.
Payment processing time is primary; envelope time is the fallback.
Envelope ingestion time identifies the inventory adjustment instant.
Detection time is primary; envelope time is the fallback.
Envelope ingestion time identifies the reorder decision instant.
Envelope ingestion time is the sensor observation time.
Payload transition time is primary; envelope time is the fallback.
Envelope ingestion time is the BLE observation time.
Arrival time starts the truck lifecycle.
Departure time completes the truck lifecycle.
Operation time records when the store opened.
Operation time records when the store closed.
Envelope ingestion time is the marketing touch time.
Envelope ingestion time orders promotion application.
Envelope ingestion time is the online order creation time.
Picked time is primary; envelope time is the fallback.
Shipped time is primary; envelope time is the fallback.

data_contracts.name: Historical Lakehouse contract
data_contracts.description: Declaration pointing to generated historical table schemas.

inventories.description: Historical table names derived from schemas.py.

data_contracts.name: Active semantic model contract
data_contracts.description: Declaration pointing to active tables referenced by model.tmdl.

inventories.description: Active semantic-model tables derived from model.tmdl references.

event_paths.name: Receipt created to reporting
event_paths.description: Typed receipt creation flows through Eventhouse and Silver to sales and tender reporting.

event_paths.name: Receipt line to product reporting
event_paths.description: Typed receipt lines flow to the receipt-line fact and top-product aggregate.

event_paths.name: Payment to payment reporting
event_paths.description: Typed payments flow to the active payment fact.

event_paths.name: Inventory update to store position
event_paths.description: Store inventory adjustments feed the current store inventory aggregate.

event_paths.name: Stockout to stockout reporting
event_paths.description: Typed stockout detections flow to the active stockout fact.

event_paths.name: Reorder to replenishment reporting
event_paths.description: Typed reorder decisions flow to the active reorder fact.

event_paths.name: Customer entry to traffic reporting
event_paths.description: Customer entry observations feed foot traffic and zone dwell reporting.

event_paths.name: Customer zone transition to reporting
event_paths.description: Typed zone transitions flow to the active customer-zone fact.

event_paths.name: BLE ping to sensor reporting
event_paths.description: Typed BLE observations flow to the active BLE ping fact.

event_paths.name: Truck arrival lifecycle route
event_paths.description: Truck arrival joins its departure before truck dwell reaches reporting.

event_paths.name: Truck departure lifecycle route
event_paths.description: Truck departure joins its arrival before truck dwell reaches reporting.

event_paths.name: Store open to operations reporting
event_paths.description: Store-open operations flow to the active store-operations fact.

event_paths.name: Store close to operations reporting
event_paths.description: Store-close operations flow to the active store-operations fact.

event_paths.name: Ad impression to campaign reporting
event_paths.description: Typed ad impressions feed marketing cost and the derived attribution route.

event_paths.name: Promotion to promotion reporting
event_paths.description: Typed applied promotions flow to the active promotion fact.

event_paths.name: Online order creation to sales reporting
event_paths.description: Created online orders feed order headers and daily online sales.

event_paths.name: Online order picked operational status
event_paths.description: Picked status stops at the intentionally streaming-only status table.

event_paths.name: Online order shipped operational status
event_paths.description: Shipped status stops at the intentionally streaming-only status table.

event_paths.name: Derived last-touch marketing attribution
event_paths.description: Five reconciled event routes derive attribution and campaign performance without a nineteenth event.

exceptions.name: KQL unknown-event catch-all
exceptions.description: KQL intentionally has a catch-all table that is not a generated business event.
exceptions.rationale: Queued raw JSON needs a bounded investigation table, while the direct typed writer must reject unmapped events before checkpoint commit.

exceptions.name: Streaming-only online order status
exceptions.description: Picked and shipped events intentionally stop at a streaming-only Silver status table.
exceptions.rationale: The active transform records operational PICKED/SHIPPED state, but no historical schema or semantic table currently owns that status grain.

exceptions.name: Historical-only Lakehouse targets
exceptions.description: Source-derived historical targets without an emitted-event path remain batch-owned.
exceptions.rationale: Dimensions and batch-only facts have no emitted payload, and DC inventory reporting depends on the historical DC inventory fact.

exceptions.name: Semantic-model ML outputs
exceptions.description: The active model intentionally includes ML outputs outside the historical base contract.
exceptions.rationale: Four contract-validated required ML outputs intentionally extend the base historical generator schema.

ml_contracts.name: Demand Forecast
ml_contracts.description: Demand Forecast required support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Capacity and replenishment planning from store/product daily unit forecasts.
limitations: Forecasts are model estimates rather than guaranteed demand.
Intervals are empirical residual bands and do not express causal uncertainty.
MAPE is a unit ratio and can exceed 1.0 when absolute percentage error exceeds 100%.
Evaluation eligibility is frozen at the training cutoff; production inference selects a separate current cohort.

ml_contracts.name: Customer Segments
ml_contracts.description: Customer Segments required support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Descriptive customer grouping for aggregate RFM analysis.
limitations: Cluster labels are descriptive and may change between runs.
Segments must not be used as protected-class or eligibility decisions.

ml_contracts.name: Churn Predictions
ml_contracts.description: Churn Predictions required support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Prioritize customers for retention analysis within the declared churn horizon.
limitations: Current inference intentionally has no actual outcome label; deprecated is_churned_actual is always null for backward compatibility.
Scores are calibrated estimates and require later outcome monitoring.
Training, calibration, and test partition on label availability with a 90-day forward-label embargo.

ml_contracts.name: Stockout Risk
ml_contracts.description: Stockout Risk required support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Rank current store/product positions for inventory review by horizon.
limitations: Scores depend on the captured inventory snapshot and observed demand history.
A high score is not a guaranteed future stockout.
Training, calibration, and test partition on label availability with a three-day forward-label embargo.

ml_contracts.name: Product Associations
ml_contracts.description: Product Associations optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Explore co-purchased product sets from qualifying baskets.
limitations: Association does not imply causation.
Sparse baskets can yield no promoted output rows.

ml_contracts.name: Product Recommendations
ml_contracts.description: Product Recommendations optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Provide deduplicated product-pair recommendations from association rules.
limitations: Recommendations inherit observational association-rule bias.
Metrics are not calibrated purchase probabilities.
Each recommendation is a singleton-pair rule whose support, confidence, and lift come from that same rule.

ml_contracts.name: Journey Patterns
ml_contracts.description: Journey Patterns optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Describe common in-store zone paths and identity-safe conversion aggregates.
limitations: Receipt conversion is present only when identity mapping is unambiguous.
Paths are descriptive and not causal.

ml_contracts.name: Zone Transitions
ml_contracts.description: Zone Transitions optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
limitations: Transition shares are empirical frequencies, not individual forecasts.

ml_contracts.name: Zone Dwell Stats
ml_contracts.description: Zone Dwell Stats optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
limitations: Outlier filtering excludes extremely long observed dwell intervals.

ml_contracts.name: Dwell Predictions
ml_contracts.description: Dwell Predictions optional support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Estimate dwell for open arrived shipments using arrival-known features.
limitations: Training labels come from matched cusn Bronze arrivals and departures; inference uses unmatched arrivals and arrival-known fields only.
Inference excludes actual duration, departure time, and error fields.
Intervals are empirical and the output is promoted only for operational review.
Missing lifecycle sources or no inference-ready open arrivals fail before replacing prior output.

ml_contracts.name: Price Elasticity
ml_contracts.description: Price Elasticity experimental support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Explore observational price-demand sensitivity with evidence diagnostics.
limitations: Observational OLS estimates are not causal price effects.
Invalid or insufficient evidence remains explicit and nullable.

ml_contracts.name: Promotion Lift
ml_contracts.description: Promotion Lift experimental support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Compare descriptive before/during/after promotion-period outcomes by store and episode.
limitations: Observed period changes are not causal lift or ROI.
Zero-sales calendar days remain in denominators only inside each store's observed receipt range.
Episodes without complete observed baseline and post windows are excluded.

ml_contracts.name: Pricing Constraints
ml_contracts.description: Pricing Constraints experimental support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Expose the rule constraints applied by experimental pricing.
limitations: Constraints are demo configuration and require operator review.

ml_contracts.name: Pricing Recommendations
ml_contracts.description: Pricing Recommendations experimental support-tier producer and physical output contract.
ml_contracts.intended_use: Explore constrained product-level pricing recommendations with upstream evidence.
limitations: Recommendations are experimental and are never required by Reporting.
No calibrated confidence model exists; ml_confidence is intentionally nullable.
Missing elasticity uses a non-null NO_ELASTICITY_ESTIMATE evidence sentinel.
Cooldown state advances only when a constrained price change is accepted.
Quantity scenarios apply the log-log power response and are not causal forecasts.
